candidate_summary: ANGKLUNG-OCTAVE: pair-level top-k MoE routing where N experts are grouped into N/2 pairs (E_a, E_b), router selects a pair (not single expert), both pair members fire simultaneously at half capacity each, and FFN weights within a pair are tied via a FIXED 'octave permutation' operator W_b = O W_a producing structured 2-octave output.

results_analysis.title: Pangu Pro MoE: Mixture of Grouped Experts (MoGE, 2025/05)
results_analysis.reason: MoGE explicitly groups experts and routes by forcing equal activations within each predefined group — directly realizes pair-level (K=2 special case) top-k routing, the central novelty axis of ANGKLUNG-OCTAVE.

results_analysis.title: Mixture of Heterogeneous Grouped Experts (MoHGE, 2026/04)
results_analysis.reason: MoHGE performs two-level routing over heterogeneous expert groups — same architectural class as pair-level routing claimed novel by ANGKLUNG-OCTAVE.

results_analysis.title: Upcycling LLMs into MoE (arxiv 2024/10)
kw_matches: weight permutation over FFN (fixed permutation expert pair operator)
FFN copies into experts (octave-coupled FFN weight tie)
results_analysis.reason: Upcycling uses a fixed weight permutation operator applied to FFN before copying into expert slots — directly the 'fixed permutation operator' relating experts that ANGKLUNG claims as a novel component.

results_analysis.title: Weight-Ensembling Mixture of Experts (arxiv 2024/10)
kw_matches: weight ensembling/sharing across experts (FFN weight tie)
results_analysis.reason: Weight-Ensembling MoE shares/ties weights across experts via a structured combiner — same artefact (tied FFN weights between experts) ANGKLUNG calls novel.

results_analysis.title: Omni-Router: Sharing Routing Decisions in Sparse MoE (2025/07)
kw_matches: shared routing in sparse MoE (pair-level top-k MoE generic match)
results_analysis.reason: Cross-layer router sharing is a different axis, but sem cosine 0.72 (>=0.7) registers a hit; functionally it remains a 'shared structure in MoE routing' adjacent to ANGKLUNG's mechanism.

results_analysis.title: Improving MoE Compute Efficiency by Composing Weight and Data Sparsity (2026/01)
results_analysis.reason: Weight+data sparsity composition is an orthogonal compute-efficiency mechanism with no pair-routing or permutation-tie content; all three scores below threshold.

results_analysis.title: Stabilizing MoE RL by Aligning Training and Inference Routers (2025/10)
results_analysis.reason: Router train/inference alignment under RL is unrelated to pair-level routing or weight-tied experts; clearly below thresholds.

results_analysis.title: LLaMA-MoE v2 Exploring Sparsity (2024/11)
results_analysis.reason: General post-training sparsity exploration without grouped/paired routing or permutation tying; below all thresholds.

verdict_sentence: ANGKLUNG-OCTAVE fails verification: 5 distinct prior works hit (MoGE pair/group routing, MoHGE two-level group routing, Upcycling fixed weight permutation over FFN copies, Weight-Ensembling MoE for tied expert weights, and Omni-Router shared-routing MoE), so each of the three claimed components (pair-level routing, fixed permutation weight tie, shared/structured pair) and their conjunction lacks novelty.
